name: Competitive Landscape

competitors.name: Splitwise
validated_capabilities: receipt scanning and currency conversion in Pro
competitors.crewly_gap_or_opportunity: Splitwise validates expense splitting but does not provide a trip-first public support surface or organizer-controlled campaign model.

competitors.name: GoFundMe
validated_capabilities: fund receipt by organizer or beneficiary
competitors.crewly_gap_or_opportunity: GoFundMe validates fundraising behavior but does not solve private trip coordination, trip ledgers, receipt localization, or private event scope separation.

competitors.name: Wanderlog
competitors.crewly_gap_or_opportunity: Wanderlog validates trip planning and budgeting demand but does not center pooled support, receipt-backed ledgers, or private event financial separation.

positioning_summary.validated_differentiator: Crewly is differentiated only if the architecture keeps funding, trip ledger transparency, private event ledgers, and media/update privacy in one coherent trip hub.
positioning_summary.saturation_reason: Each component exists in adjacent tools, so generic trip planning or generic expense splitting would be weak positioning.

recommendations: Avoid competing as a generic itinerary planner or generic expense splitter.
Make ledger scope, public contribution trust, and receipt-backed transparency first-class product claims.
Do not add in-app private event settlement in MVP; it would move Crewly closer to crowded P2P settlement complexity without proving the core wedge.
